business.name: Sage & Stone Landscape Studio
business.tagline: Yards designed for how you live outside
business.oneLineDescription: A landscape design and build studio in Austin creating considered, low-water outdoor spaces — from first sketch to installed garden — for homeowners across the Texas Hill Country.

contact.serviceArea: Austin, Westlake, Lakeway, Dripping Springs & the Texas Hill Country

audience.customerDescription: Homeowners who want more than a mowed lawn — an outdoor space that's beautiful, water-wise, and built to last. People buying design and craft, who want to see the plan before the first shovel goes in.

topQuestions.question: How much does a landscape design cost?
topQuestions.answer: Our design packages start at $2,500 for a single area and scale with the size and complexity of your property. Design is a fixed fee you approve before we begin, and it includes a site visit, a full plan, a planting palette, and renderings so you can see the finished garden before anything is built.

topQuestions.question: Do you build what you design, or just draw it?
topQuestions.answer: Both. We're a design-and-build studio, so the same team that designs your garden installs it — hardscape, drainage, planting, irrigation, and lighting. You can also take our plan to your own contractor; most clients prefer we see it through so the built result matches the drawing.

topQuestions.question: How long does a project take?
topQuestions.answer: Design typically takes 3–5 weeks from the first site visit to an approved plan. Build timelines depend on scope — a refreshed front yard might be a week, a full backyard transformation four to eight. We give you a realistic schedule with your proposal, not a vague promise.

topQuestions.question: Do you design for drought and Texas heat?
topQuestions.answer: Always. We lead with native and adapted, low-water plants, efficient drip irrigation, and materials that handle full Texas sun — so your garden looks intentional in August, not just in April, and your water bill stays reasonable.

topQuestions.question: What areas do you serve?
topQuestions.answer: We work across Austin, Westlake, Lakeway, Dripping Springs, and the surrounding Hill Country. If you're a little outside that range, reach out — we'll tell you honestly whether we're the right fit for your project.

services.name: Landscape design
services.description: A considered, buildable plan — layout, planting palette, materials, and renderings — so you can see and shape your garden before a shovel touches the ground.

services.name: Full-service build & install
services.description: Our own crews bring the plan to life: hardscape, patios, drainage, planting, irrigation, and lighting — finished to a standard that lasts.

services.name: Native & drought-tolerant planting
services.description: Low-water, Hill-Country-adapted planting designed for year-round structure and color that thrives through the Texas summer.

services.name: Pools & water features
services.description: Pools, plunge pools, fountains, and rills designed as part of the whole garden — not bolted on afterward.

services.name: Garden care & maintenance
services.description: Optional seasonal care that keeps your garden looking its best as it matures — pruning, bed refreshes, and irrigation checks.

hero.imageAlt: A designed backyard garden at golden hour with native planting and a stone patio
hero.headline: Yards designed for how you live outside
hero.subhead: Sage & Stone is an Austin design-and-build studio creating considered, water-wise outdoor spaces — from the first sketch to the finished garden.

process.heading: From first call to finished garden
process.subhead: A clear, four-step path — you always know what happens next and what it costs.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We walk your space, talk through how you want to live outside, and set a budget that's honest and real.

steps.title: Design
steps.description: A considered plan — layout, planting, and materials — with renderings so you can see it before we build.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Our crews install it right: hardscape, drainage, planting, and lighting, finished to last.

steps.title: Grow
steps.description: Optional seasonal care keeps the garden looking its best as it settles in and matures.

gallery.heading: Gardens we've designed and built
gallery.subhead: A few recent projects across Austin and the Hill Country.

about.imageAlt: A Sage & Stone designer planting in a finished garden
about.heading: Design-led, build-ready
about.body: Sage & Stone started with a simple belief: a garden should be designed with the same care as the house it belongs to. We're a small studio of designers and builders who see every project through — so the garden you approve on paper is the one you live in. We lead with native planting, honest budgets, and craft that gets better with every season.

testimonials.quote: They listened before they drew anything. The renderings meant there were zero surprises, and the finished yard looks even better than the plan. We use it every single evening.
testimonials.author: Elena & Marco V.

testimonials.quote: Our water bill dropped and the garden looks incredible in July, which around here is the real test. Sage & Stone actually design for Texas, not for a magazine in Oregon.
testimonials.author: Dana P.

testimonials.quote: One team from sketch to install meant nothing got lost in translation. Thoughtful people, beautiful work, and they showed up when they said they would.
testimonials.author: The Okafor family
